findings.evidence: main.ts line 148: function showBiomePicker(container: HTMLElement): Promise<string>
main.ts line 217: async function runMerlinLair(app: HTMLElement): Promise<string>. Returns selectedBiomeId (string) at line 345.

findings.item: while(true) loop uses chosenBiome in startRun()
findings.evidence: main.ts line 376: const chosenBiome = await runMerlinLair(app). Line 420: store.getState().startRun(chosenBiome).

findings.item: while(true) loop uses chosenBiome in showBiomeToast()

findings.evidence: main.ts lines 307-330: zone === 'map' branch awaits showBiomePicker(wrapper!) and stores result in selectedBiomeId.

findings.analysis: cursorX=190, cursorY=190 initialized at field declaration (line 34-35) AND re-set in setup() at lines 100-101. The zone also starts at zoneX=190, zoneY=190. isInside is initialized to true at line 44 AND reset to true in setup() at line 110. On game start, cursor is co-located with zone center, so dist=0 < startRadius=120: isInside=true. The zone drifts away over time, but cursor stays at (190,190). The zone drifts only ±18px/s from (190,190). With startRadius=120 shrinking to 28 over 10s, the zone must travel ~92px from center before cursor falls outside. At 18px/s max drift, the zone escapes the cursor after roughly 5-6 seconds. Idle player scores approximately 50-60% (time inside before zone drifts out of range), not 0. This is a significant exploit: an idle player gets half-credit without moving.

cursorX=190, cursorY=190 initialized at field declaration (lines 44-45) AND re-set in setup() at lines 108-109. isOnTarget initialized to true at line 49 AND reset to true in setup() at line 113. Target center is fixed at (centerX=190, centerY=190). Cursor stays at (190,190) for idle player. dist = sqrt(0^2+0^2) = 0, which is <= targetRadius=30, so isOnTarget=true for the entire 10 seconds. Idle player scores 100%.
findings.verdict: FAIL — idle player scores 100% without ever moving

check_2_idle_exploit.root_cause: Both minigames initialize cursorX=190, cursorY=190 which coincides exactly with the game zone center. The cursor position is set before any pointer event fires. The score accumulates from frame 0 because isInside/isOnTarget starts true and cursor is already inside the target zone.
check_2_idle_exploit.recommendation: Initialize cursorX and cursorY to a position outside the target zone, e.g. cursorX=-9999, cursorY=-9999, and initialize isInside=false / isOnTarget=false. This forces a score of 0 until the player actually moves the cursor onto the canvas.

new_bugs_found.issue: Idle cursor exploit — scores 100% without any input
new_bugs_found.detail: cursorX=190/cursorY=190 starts at exact center. targetRadius=30. dist=0. isOnTarget=true from frame 0. Player idles for 10s = 100% score.
new_bugs_found.recommendation: Initialize cursorX/cursorY to -9999 and isOnTarget=false before setup(). Only update on first pointer event.

new_bugs_found.issue: Idle cursor exploit — scores ~50-60% without any input
new_bugs_found.detail: cursorX=190/cursorY=190 starts co-located with zone center (190,190). Zone drifts at max 18px/s, startRadius=120. Idle player stays inside for several seconds before zone drifts out of reach. Score is not 0.
new_bugs_found.recommendation: Initialize cursorX/cursorY to -9999 and isInside=false. Also note: the zone drift is fully random and seeded at setup(), meaning with favorable drift the idle player could stay inside significantly longer than the worst case.

new_bugs_found.issue: BIOME_LABELS map referenced but not confirmed defined in this file
new_bugs_found.detail: Line 310 uses BIOME_LABELS[selectedBiomeId] to generate a human-readable label for the confirmation toast. If BIOME_LABELS is undefined or missing a key, the confirmation toast silently shows the raw biome ID (e.g. 'foret_broceliande') instead of the French label. Not a crash, but a UX defect.
new_bugs_found.recommendation: Verify BIOME_LABELS is defined in scope (same file or import). Fallback is already in place via ?? selectedBiomeId, so severity is MEDIUM.

new_bugs_found.issue: render() uses fixed dt=1/60 instead of real elapsed time
new_bugs_found.detail: Line 155: const dt = 1/60 hardcoded. If the browser drops below 60fps (mobile, tab backgrounded), game time accumulates faster than wall clock. Zone shrinks faster and drift is faster than intended. Not a crash but causes inconsistent difficulty on low-end devices.
new_bugs_found.recommendation: Pass real dt from rAF timestamp, same pattern as SceneManager. Low priority as minigames are short (10s).

new_bugs_found.issue: Same fixed dt=1/60 issue as mg_sang_froid
new_bugs_found.detail: Line 182: const dt = 1/60 hardcoded. Same consequences as sang_froid.

summary.check_2_idle_exploit: FAIL — both minigames vulnerable to idle cursor exploit
summary.action_required: Fix idle cursor initialization in mg_sang_froid.ts and mg_volonte.ts before shipping C74

balance_recommendations.reason: Forces cursor outside zone until player actually moves mouse/finger
